content.body: This document exists to define the recommended production layout for UX JSON corpuses. Use it when creating module, submodule, implied-submodule, or shared App Behavior specs that need to be scriptable, reviewable, and database-compatible.

content.title: Recommended Corpus Layout
body: Use this structure for a coherent UX owner:
`ux-corpus.json` is the manifest. It should identify the corpus owner, governing JSON files, generated review outputs, split/compose policy, and related parent or child corpuses.

content.title: Recursive Owner Folder Default
body: UX/App Behavior storage defaults to recursive owner folders. When creating a module, submodule, implied-submodule, shared behavior, generated-output, proof, or validation artifact home, create or use the smallest durable owner folder that can grow with related files.

Runtime/source files governed or planned from App Behavior still follow the root and engineering runtime ownership rules. Components, hooks, services, adapters, controllers, scripts, configs, fixtures, tests, generated-output homes, and implementation helpers should be routed to the smallest durable source owner folder, and file-level tests should be colocated with the exact file they test.
This applies even when v1 has only one file. Do not create a flat shared behavior or UX artifact file in a broad parent folder merely because the first version is small.

Owner folders should carry a README, manifest, index, or equivalent routing surface when they are durable. Parent folders route and index; child owner folders contain the behavior substance and related evidence/proof artifacts.

Flat files are allowed only for root locators/indexes, compatibility-required paths, existing legacy compatibility files, temporary scratch output, or truly standalone artifacts where recursion adds no ownership, routing, lifecycle, proof, or expansion value.

content.title: Authoring Default
body: Use collection files by default because they are easier to review and manage:

Each item inside the collection must still be portable. A script should be able to split one item into a smaller JSON file or compose several item files back into a collection without changing meaning.

content.title: Split File Compatibility
body: Split files should keep the same item schema:
Do not invent a different schema for split files. File layout is a storage choice, not a semantic model.

content.title: Generated Markdown
content.body: Generated Markdown belongs under `generated/` and is review-only. It should name the governing JSON source and state that manual edits will be overwritten.

content.title: Shared UX Fallback
content.body: Place App Behavior corpuses inside the owning module or implied submodule when possible. Use shared UX folders only when no clear module or submodule owner exists.
